# webauthn-server-buildkit

> A comprehensive, framework-independent WebAuthn / FIDO2 / passkeys **server**
> library for TypeScript and Node.js. It verifies registration and
> authentication ceremonies, performs full per-format attestation verification
> with FIDO Metadata Service (MDS) trust anchoring, mints encrypted session
> tokens, and persists data through pluggable storage adapters. Secure by
> default, zero framework lock-in, one runtime dependency (cbor-x).
> Author: Ahsan Mahmood (https://aoneahsan.com). License: MIT.

This file follows the https://llmstxt.org convention so language models can find
the most useful, machine-readable entry points for this project.

## What it does

- WebAuthn registration ceremony: generate `PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions`,
  verify the attestation response (challenge, origin, RP-ID hash, flags,
  signature, single-use challenge).
- WebAuthn authentication ceremony: generate
  `PublicKeyCredentialRequestOptions`, verify the assertion (signature, counter,
  user-handle binding, algorithm pinning).
- Attestation: `none`, `packed` (self + x5c), `fido-u2f`, `android-key`, `tpm`,
  `android-safetynet`, and `apple` are cryptographically verified.
  `attestationVerified: true` is reported only when trust-anchored (apple via a
  bundled root CA; the rest via an RP-supplied FIDO MDS `MetadataService`).
- Encrypted session tokens: AES-256-GCM with HKDF-SHA256 key derivation,
  managed entirely behind the `WebAuthnServer` API.
- Storage: a built-in in-memory adapter plus a `StorageAdapter` interface for
  Postgres, Redis, MongoDB, or any backend.

## Key URLs

- Documentation home: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com
- Installation: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com/getting-started/installation
- Quick start: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com/getting-started/quick-start
- API reference (WebAuthnServer): https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com/api/webauthn-server
- Configuration reference: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com/api/configuration
- Storage adapter reference: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com/api/storage-adapter
- Attestation guide: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com/guides/attestation
- Security model: https://webauthn-server-buildkit-docs.aoneahsan.com/guides/security-model
- npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/webauthn-server-buildkit
- Source code: https://github.com/aoneahsan/webauthn-server-buildkit

## Honest scope notes

- This is a **server** library. The browser side (`navigator.credentials`) and
  the native side are out of scope; pair it with a WebAuthn client and, on
  Capacitor, with `capacitor-biometric-authentication`.
- WebAuthn requires a secure context (HTTPS, or `localhost` for development).
- Attestation is offline by design: there is no CRL/OCSP revocation checking.
- The optional `enableMobileAttestation` JSON path (default OFF) is non-standard
  and does NOT perform cryptographic WebAuthn attestation — use it only when you
  fully control the client and transport.

## Author

Ahsan Mahmood — https://aoneahsan.com · https://github.com/aoneahsan ·
https://www.npmjs.com/~aoneahsan
